description: PTQ: quantization applied after training with no gradient updates. Scale S and zero-point Z derived from calibration data. Three back-ends: dynamic (no calib), static FX sweep (4 observers), FX-graph with backend-tuned default qconfigs.

ptq_math.note: INT8 works well with PTQ; INT4 degrades because 15 levels gives large S → large |ε|, and errors accumulate through layers with no mechanism to correct them (that is QAT's job).

results.description: Dynamic PTQ: only Linear weights quantized to qint8. S and Z computed on-the-fly per batch — no calibration data. Conv2d stays FP32. Fast to apply; no memory of x_min/x_max needed.

quantization_formulas.S: computed per-batch at runtime from weight range

results.description: Static PTQ (FX-graph, minmax): graph-traced fusion of Conv-BN-ReLU and residual adds. S and Z calibrated from 1024 training images. Weights and activations quantized to INT8.

quantization_formulas.max_err: |ε| ≤ S/2  →  smaller S = finer grid = less error

results.description: Static PTQ (FX-graph, moving_avg): graph-traced fusion of Conv-BN-ReLU and residual adds. S and Z calibrated from 1024 training images. Weights and activations quantized to INT8.

quantization_formulas.max_err: |ε| ≤ S/2  →  smaller S = finer grid = less error

results.description: Static PTQ (FX-graph, histogram): graph-traced fusion of Conv-BN-ReLU and residual adds. S and Z calibrated from 1024 training images. Weights and activations quantized to INT8.

quantization_formulas.max_err: |ε| ≤ S/2  →  smaller S = finer grid = less error

results.description: Static PTQ (FX-graph, percentile): graph-traced fusion of Conv-BN-ReLU and residual adds. S and Z calibrated from 1024 training images. Weights and activations quantized to INT8.

quantization_formulas.max_err: |ε| ≤ S/2  →  smaller S = finer grid = less error

results.description: FX-graph static PTQ (backend=x86): torch.ao traces the computation graph, automatically fuses Conv-BN-ReLU and quantizes all eligible ops including residual adds. Same S/Z formulas as eager static; backend-tuned qconfig.

quantization_formulas.throughput: INT8 VNNI ~4× FP32 on AVX-512 CPUs

results.description: FX-graph static PTQ (backend=fbgemm): torch.ao traces the computation graph, automatically fuses Conv-BN-ReLU and quantizes all eligible ops including residual adds. Same S/Z formulas as eager static; backend-tuned qconfig.

quantization_formulas.throughput: INT8 VNNI ~4× FP32 on AVX-512 CPUs

notes.dynamic: No calibration; Linear only; fastest to apply; Conv stays FP32
notes.static_fx: Calibration required; best INT8 accuracy; Conv-BN-ReLU auto-fused by FX
notes.fx_default: Backend-tuned qconfig; broadest op coverage; model must be torch.fx-traceable
notes.gpu_note: PyTorch static PTQ is CPU-only; GPU INT8 → TensorRT / ONNX Runtime
notes.speedup_note: INT8 ops ~2-4× faster than FP32 on CPUs with AVX-512 VNNI support
notes.vs_qat: PTQ requires no retraining and is fast; QAT closes the accuracy gap at INT4 by inserting fake-quant nodes during training and using the STE trick.
notes.disk_size_note: disk_size_mb uses torch.save(model) (full model, not state_dict) to ensure INT8 tensor packing is preserved in the serialized file.
